decontamination_notes: Real security bug fix from colinhacks/zod PR #5898, merged 2026-04-29, after model training cutoffs. In handleCatchall (packages/zod/src/v4/core/schemas.ts) an unrecognized __proto__ key from input was assigned via result[key]=value, going through the __proto__ setter and replacing the fresh result object's prototype (prototype pollution). The fix adds `if (key === '__proto__') continue;` to the catchall iteration. Gold behavior was captured empirically in the node-ts sandbox: __proto__ dropped from own keys, prototype stays Object.prototype, no global leak, and a strictObject given a __proto__ key parses successfully (since it is no longer collected as unrecognized). Zod is a zero-runtime-dependency TS monorepo with extensionless ESM imports; hidden tests run via tsx (node-ts image) with node:test. JSON.parse is used in tests to create the real own __proto__ property (an object literal's __proto__: is the setter, not the vector). Repo sliced at the PR base commit (67k LOC); MIT LICENSE preserved. Coverage floor: 3 fail_to_pass tests, each of which FAILS at the unfixed base (result prototype replaced, attacker payload inherited, strictObject rejects __proto__) plus a regression guard covering ordinary behavior and the own-keys invariant that holds both before and after.

fail_to_pass.name: clean_prototype
fail_to_pass.cmd: tsx --test --test-name-pattern='^vb catchall result keeps a clean prototype$' vb_proto_catchall.test.ts

fail_to_pass.name: no_payload_exposed
fail_to_pass.cmd: tsx --test --test-name-pattern='^vb catchall does not expose the attacker payload$' vb_proto_catchall.test.ts

fail_to_pass.name: strict_proto_ok
fail_to_pass.cmd: tsx --test --test-name-pattern='^vb strictObject with a __proto__ key parses successfully$' vb_proto_catchall.test.ts

pass_to_pass.name: ordinary_unaffected
pass_to_pass.cmd: tsx --test --test-name-pattern='^vb ordinary catchall and strict behavior unaffected$' vb_regression.test.ts
